meta.description: Expand character tokens and generate final prompts for all SSD scenes

step.name: load_ssd_and_references
step.action: Load the SSD, all character identity files, and the style guide. For each character referenced in the SSD, load their prompt_tokens (core_identity, style_modifiers, negative_tokens, consistency_anchors). Load the style guide's style tokens (master prefix, scene-type tokens) and negative prompts (global, character, style, quality).
step.output: SSD, character tokens, and style tokens loaded

step.name: expand_character_placeholders
step.action: For each scene/clip prompt in the SSD, find all [character:X] placeholders and expand them to the character's full core_identity tokens. Place expanded character tokens at the front of the prompt (front-loading) since AI models weight earlier tokens more heavily. Also expand [outfit:Y] placeholders to the corresponding outfit's prompt_tokens from the character's active style.
step.output: Prompts with all character and outfit placeholders expanded

step.name: prepend_style_tokens
step.action: For each expanded prompt, prepend the appropriate style tokens: master style prefix first, then scene-type specific tokens (intro, content, transition, outro). Ensure style tokens enhance but do not contradict scene-specific visual directions already in the prompt.

step.name: append_negative_prompts
step.action: For each prompt, build the complete negative prompt by combining: global negative prompts from the style guide, character-specific negative tokens for each character in the scene, style-specific negatives, and quality negatives. Deduplicate negative tokens and order by importance.
step.output: Prompts with complete negative prompt sections

step.name: validate_token_limits
step.action: For each final prompt (positive + negative), count tokens and verify the total is under 1024 tokens. If any prompt exceeds the limit: first try removing redundant style tokens, then try condensing character descriptions, then flag for manual review if still over. Log the token count for each prompt.

step.name: validate_camera_movements
step.action: For each clip prompt, verify that only one camera movement is specified (pan, zoom, dolly, static, etc.). Multiple camera movements in a single clip cause AI generation artifacts. If conflicts are found, keep the primary movement and remove or split the secondary into a separate clip suggestion.

step.name: validate_token_ordering
step.action: For each prompt, verify that character identity tokens appear before scene description tokens, which appear before style modifier tokens. This front-loading order ensures the most important visual elements receive the strongest model attention. Reorder if necessary.

step.name: generate_prompt_library
step.action: Compile all final prompts into a prompt library document: for each scene/clip, record the scene_id, clip_id, final_positive_prompt, final_negative_prompt, token_count, characters_referenced, style_tokens_applied, and a prompt_hash (for cache/dedup purposes). Save as episodes/{episode_id}/prompts/prompt-library.yaml.

step.name: update_ssd_and_cross_reference
step.action: Update the SSD with references to the prompt library entries for each scene/clip. Cross-reference all prompts for consistency: same character should have similar token patterns across scenes, style tokens should be consistent, and no contradictory directions between adjacent scenes. Flag any inconsistencies.
step.output: Updated SSD with prompt library references and consistency report

quality-gates.gate: All [character:X] and [outfit:Y] placeholders are fully expanded
All prompts are under 1024 tokens (positive + negative combined)
Character identity tokens are front-loaded in every prompt
Negative prompts are present for every scene/clip
No conflicting camera movements within a single clip
Prompt consistency verified across scenes for same characters
Prompt library saved and cross-referenced with SSD
